items.title: Futures Settle: Natural Gas Prices Surrender Iran War Gains
items.summary: Natural gas futures roared to life Sunday night as the war in the Middle East rattled global energy markets, climbing toward $3.500/MMBtu before surrendering those gains and more by Monday’s close.

items.title: MidDay Snapshot: Spot Gas Mixed on Weather Demand; Henry Hub Gains on LNG Strength
items.summary: Spot gas markets were mixed Monday as weather outlooks varied, while Middle East tensions and near-record LNG feed gas demand lent support to the benchmark Henry Hub.

items.title: Esentia, Siemens Partner to Boost Waha-to-Mexico Gas Flow to 2 Bcf/d
items.summary: West Texas natural gas producers are set to have more access to the Mexican market after Esentia Energy Systems and Siemens Energy AG agreed to upgrade the Wahalajara system.

items.title: Global Natural Gas Prices Skyrocket Amid Intensifying Middle East War
items.summary: Global natural gas prices soared Monday, extending their biggest gains since the energy crisis of 2022 as conflict in the Middle East that has cut off a fifth of the world’s oil and gas supplies shows no signs of ending soon.

items.title: MidDay Futures: Natural Gas Rally Fades as Mild Weather Counters Geopolitical Risk
items.summary: Natural gas futures rallied early Monday but the advance faded by midday, with the April contract retreating from intraday highs near $3.50/MMBtu as domestic fundamentals offset geopolitical concerns in the Middle East. Despite the pullback, the broa

items.title: Midmorning Markets: Amid War Wildcards, Technical Ceilings Rise for Natural Gas Futures
items.summary: After the war in Iran cut global energy supply, prompt month natural gas futures pushed through technical resistance levels and set new barriers early this week.

items.title: Falling Natural Gas Prices Drive Coal-to-Gas Switching Surge
items.summary: Natural gas has grabbed back share of the U.S. thermal generation stack over the past month as plunging spot prices triggered a wave of coal-to-gas switching.

items.title: Canadian Natural Calls for More LNG After Record Production Year
items.summary: Canadian Natural Resources Ltd. navigated a complex natural gas market in 2025 and had the “best operational year” in its history despite structural challenges.

items.title: Energy Equities Mixed Despite Oil’s Biggest Rally Since 2022
items.summary: Oil prices briefly hit triple-digits in early Monday trading before pulling back sharply, with the volatility throwing global energy stocks into a tizzy. Brent crude oil rocketed to $119 during Asia Pacific trading, its highest level since 2022, befo

items.title: Oil Shock Spreads Through Global Economy
items.summary: Brent crude and WTI are once again above $100 and likely to stay there longer than those involved in the planning of the latest war in the Middle East may have expected. With that, energy-thirsty economies are beginning to feel the pinch, and not eve

items.title: G7 Nations Delay Strategic Oil Reserve Release Decision
items.summary: Finance ministers from the Group of Seven (G7) countries reached a broad agreement on Monday to hold off the release of oil from their respective strategic reserves, for now. The ministers held a teleconference on Monday after oil prices spiked to le

items.title: Oil Price Shock Could Worsen If U.S. Seizes Iran’s Strategic Oil Island
items.summary: JP Morgan has warned that Iran’s oil production could be slashed in half and oil exports could virtually stall if U.S.-Israeli seize Iran's Kharg Island, worsening the ongoing global oil shock. Located in the Persian Gulf, the continental island is t

items.title: Why $100 Oil Isn’t Going to Spark a New Shale Boom
items.summary: When oil hit $55 per barrel in late 2025, the drilling and completions side of the industry surrendered. A few months later, war breaks out in Iran, and WTI climbs past $100. That’s a marker at which meaningful drilling should occur. Yet, that’s not

items.title: Record Volumes of Sanctioned Oil Sit on Tankers Offshore China
items.summary: Record volumes of nearly 40 million barrels of sanctioned Iranian, Russian, and Venezuelan crude are idling in floating storage on tankers near China, according to Kpler data cited by Bloomberg on Monday.   The crude volumes in floating storage, with

items.title: Europe’s Gas Prices Continue to Soar as War Resets Supply Routes
items.summary: Europe’s benchmark natural gas prices jumped by another 30% at market open on Monday, following a massive 67% weekly gain last week, as oil prices soared past $100 per barrel and Europe remains the most vulnerable customer of LNG supply.  The April 2
